spans.text: chanting slow the solemn rite , To Gwentland's pleasant plain
With gloves of steel , and good broadsword , And plumed helm of brass , Hoel , Landoga's youthful lord , To hear the father's holy word
who will friends and home forsake
who will friends and home forsake
The sign of the Holy Cross
thy helpless years , Who dry thy widowed mother's tears
a shade overcast Brave Hoel's ruddy hue , But soon the moment's thought is past : — Hark , hark , it's the trumpet's stirring blast
a moment's gloom Sit in brave Hoel's eye
the noise , Then crouched at his master's feet
Who all my soul can see
It were one tear from thee
thou art free , To wed or not , and to think of me
cast every care to the wind , And dry , best beloved , the tear
cast every care to the wind , And dry , best beloved , the tear
the long day , where the forest-trees wave
lip the first kiss Of thy holy and innocent love
my lip the first kiss Of thy holy and innocent love
bring my boy ; may God above Shower blessings on his head !
God above Shower blessings on his head
the warrior train Of knights and barons bold
spoke the tale of seasons gone , And half-revealed its ivied stone
lonely to the cloudy height Of the steep hill she strays
The clouds were gathered on its brow
to her ear , And sudden a sweet voice
passed her by , Ere saw her dark , yet gentle eye
griefs she did impart , And tears of memory
cheek A smile of faint despair
cheek A smile of faint despair
Fortune to all thy warm hopes was unkind
with a starting tear , And trembling accent , cried , OH lady , hide that look severe , — The voice of love , of friendship hear , And be again a bride
OH lady , hide that look severe , — The voice of love , of friendship hear , And be again a bride
The voice of love , of friendship
Lady , he is dead , is dead
Sev'n years their course have rolled , Since thus brave Hoel spoke , When last I heard his voice , Behold , This ring , — it is of purest gold , — Then , keep it for my sake
thou art free , To wed or not , and to think of me As I had never been
If in that space I see no more The friend I ever must deplore , Then take a mourner's hand
no more The friend I ever must deplore , Then take a mourner's hand
a rushing torrent grey , From many a wild brook's wandering way , The hoary minstrels
the acclaiming lay , When , with descending beard , Scallop , and staff his steps to stay , As , foot-sore , on his weary way , A pilgrim won appeared
A poor man's offering to make , If haply still my hand may wake Some pleasant melody
There lies a harp upon the ground , And none hath ever heard its sound , Since my brave father died
that thou ever shalt find The friend of thy bosom sincere
The dark eyes of her Hoel
The dark eyes of her Hoel
a little space , To hide the tears of joy ; Then rushing , with a warm embrace , Cried , as he kissed young Hoel's face , My boy , my heart-loved boy
a little space , To hide the tears of joy ; Then rushing , with a warm embrace , Cried , as he kissed young Hoel's face , My boy , my heart-loved boy
rushing , with a warm embrace , Cried , as he kissed young Hoel's face , My boy , my heart-loved boy
a bride away , Accept a long-lost friend
a bride away , Accept a long-lost friend
at the close of day The village harper tuned
the song , with counsel sage , To teach one lesson to a wiser age
